title: galium trifidum subsp. pacificum
intro: Galium trifidum subsp. pacificum, also known as the Pacific bedstraw or three-leafed bedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium trifidum plant, a type of flowering plant in the bedstraw family. It is native to the Pacific coastal regions of North America, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating inflammation and wound healing due to its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and boosting the immune system. Galium trifidum subsp. pacificum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal medicine and traditional remedies.
